From San Francisco to Reno, the flagpoles are about a foot high. From Reno eastward, the poles increase in height until Columbus, Ohio, which is about four-fifths of the way across the nation. Then near the eastern terminus (New York), the poles suddenly get much taller. So the key points here are the order of the cities along this road from west to east. Columbus is mentioned as being about four-fifths of the way across the nation. Then approaching the eastern end (New York), the poles get even taller. So the order from west to east would be: San Francisco, then Reno, then Columbus, then New York.
